label: I.1
statement: P3 = x^3-x-1 is irreducible over Q

label: I.2
statement: P4 = x^4-x-1 is irreducible over Q

label: I.3
statement: gcd(P3, P4) = 1 (coprime over Z[x])

label: I.4
statement: resultant(P3, P4) = 1 (no common root in any extension)

label: I.5
statement: disc(P3) = -23 (prime)

label: I.6
statement: disc(P4) = -283 (prime)

label: I.7
statement: Identity: 1 - 1/rho = 2 - rho^2  (error < 10^-13)

label: I.8
statement: Identity: 1 - 1/Q = 2 - Q^3  (error < 10^-13)

label: I.9
statement: General identity: 1-1/r_n = 2-r_n^(n-1) for all roots of x^n=x+1

label: I.10
statement: Gal(P3/Q) = S3

label: I.11
statement: Gal(P4/Q) = S4

label: I.12
statement: [Q(rho,Q):Q] = 12 (fields linearly disjoint; follows from resultant=1)

label: II.1
statement: N(rho) = +1  (positive-norm unit in Q(rho))

label: II.2
statement: N(Q) = -1  (negative-norm unit in Q(Q))

label: II.3
statement: N(rho*Q) = N(rho) x N(Q) = -1  [THE ALGEBRAIC LOCK]

label: II.4
statement: N((rho*Q)^n) = (-1)^n for all integers n

label: II.5
statement: rho*Q is a fundamental unit in the degree-12 compositum Q(rho,Q)

label: II.6
statement: Unit group of Q(rho,Q) has rank 5 (Dirichlet's unit theorem)

label: II.7
statement: Vieta: rho*|sigma|^2 = 1, so lam3 = 1-|sigma|^2 is a theorem

label: II.8
statement: |sigma_4| = 1.06333694 > 1: Q-conjugates escape unit disk

label: II.9
statement: h(-23) = h(-283) = 3: discriminants share class number 3

label: II.10
statement: N(2Q-1) = -23 = disc(P3): bridge prime connecting Q(rho) and Q(Q)

label: II.11
statement: phi - rho*Q = 0.00089238: gap is algebraically irreducible

label: II.12
statement: Hodge star *^2=-1 on 4D 2-forms <=> N(Q)=-1: same identity, two languages

label: III.1
statement: Padovan: P(n) = P(n-2)+P(n-3), starts 1,1,1

label: III.2
statement: Perrin: A(n) = A(n-2)+A(n-3), starts 3,0,2

label: III.3
statement: Per(19) = 209 = gravity staircase tree floor (15^2-15-1=209)

label: III.4
statement: Perrin primality test: Per(p) = 0 (mod p) for all primes p

label: III.5
statement: Padovan: Pad(n)/Pad(n-1) -> rho (Pisot convergence of ratios)

label: III.6
statement: Perrin is a trace sequence: A(n) = sum of r_i^n over all 3 roots

label: III.7
statement: Q-sector: sum of q_i^n traces Q-sector dynamics (bounded, oscillatory)

label: III.8
statement: Padovan sequence counts triangulations in the plastic-constant tiling

label: IV.1
statement: 3D convergence rate |sigma|^2 = 1/rho = 0.75487767 < 1 per step

label: IV.2
statement: 4D divergence rate |sigma_4|^2 = 1.13068545 > 1 per step

label: IV.3
statement: Padovan ratio Pad(n)/Pad(n-1) converges to rho; error at n=40 < 10^-6

label: IV.4
statement: Topological entropy of P3 substitution = log(rho) = 0.28117

label: IV.5
statement: Topological entropy of P4 substitution = log(Q) = 0.19927

label: IV.6
statement: Companion matrix C3 satisfies P3: C3^3 = C3+I (Cayley-Hamilton)

label: IV.7
statement: Newton power sums for P3: p1=0, p2=2, p3=3

label: IV.8
statement: Koide ratio p2/p3 = 2/3 (lepton mass ratio from polynomial power sums)

label: IV.9
statement: n=3 is the unique member of x^n=x+1 with oscillatory Pisot convergence

label: IV.10
statement: Bombieri-Taylor criterion satisfied by P3: quasicrystal order possible

label: IV.11
statement: Bombieri-Taylor criterion fails for P4: no long-range order in 4D sector

label: V.2
statement: The staircase {(rho*Q)^n} is a cyclic subgroup of the unit group

label: V.3
statement: (rho*Q)^n has norm (-1)^n: alternating signs through the staircase

label: V.4
statement: Every staircase floor (rho*Q)^n is a unit in the ring of integers of Q(rho,Q)

label: V.5
statement: Regulator of Q(rho) subfield: R3 = log(rho) = 0.28117

label: V.6
statement: BI elliptic observation: log(rho)*kappa = 0.23879 (0.54% from 0.2375; no proved mechanism)

label: VI.1
statement: P3 defines elliptic curve: Cremona 368f1, rank 0

label: VI.2
statement: P4 defines elliptic curve: Cremona 1132b1, rank 1

label: VI.3
statement: Conductor of E3: 368 = 2^4 x 23 (encodes ramification prime of Q(rho))

label: VI.4
statement: Conductor of E4: 1132 = 2^2 x 283 (encodes ramification prime of Q(Q))

label: VI.5
statement: Gross-Zagier: alpha_s(MZ) = Omega_4/23 ~ 0.11793

label: VI.6
statement: h(-23) = h(-283) = 3: class numbers match Tamagawa number of E4

label: VI.7
statement: E3 rank 0: L(E3,1) != 0, finitely many rational points (Kolyvagin)

label: VI.8
statement: E4 rank 1: L(E4,1)=0, L'(E4,1) != 0 (Gross-Zagier formula applies)

label: VI.9
statement: Rank asymmetry (0 vs 1) mirrors Pisot/non-Pisot asymmetry of rho vs Q

label: VII.1
statement: P3-substitution: Pisot => pure discrete spectrum, unique ergodic measure

label: VII.2
statement: P4-system: non-Pisot => no pure discrete spectrum, mixing component

label: VII.3
statement: n=3 is the maximal n for which x^n=x+1 satisfies Bombieri-Taylor

label: VII.4
statement: Cosmological ringing: n*pi^2*lam_n -> pi^2*log(2) = 6.8411 HHz

label: VII.5
statement: Ringermacher-Mead (2016) supernova data: 7.14 HHz (within error bar)

label: VII.6
statement: P3-tiling is self-similar with inflation ratio rho = 1.32472

label: VIII.1
statement: 23 is the unique ramification prime of Q(rho)

label: VIII.2
statement: 283 is the unique ramification prime of Q(Q)

label: VIII.3
statement: Both 23 and 283 are prime

label: VIII.4
statement: 23 x 283 = 6509: product of the two bridge primes

label: VIII.5
statement: 368 = 16 x 23: conductor of E3 encodes ramification prime of Q(rho)

label: VIII.6
statement: 1132 = 4 x 283: conductor of E4 encodes ramification prime of Q(Q)

label: VIII.7
statement: Ramification: 23 bridges Q(rho) to Q(rho,Q) via N(2Q-1) = -23

label: IX.1
statement: Fine structure: (rho*Q)^15/pi^2 = 137.063253 (measured 137.036, error 0.020%)

label: IX.2
statement: 15 = dim(SO(4,2)) = 6+4+4+1: conformal group of 4D Minkowski

label: IX.3
statement: Helium fraction: lam3 = 0.24512 (measured 0.2449, error 0.08%)

label: IX.4
statement: Weak mixing: lam4/chi^3 = 0.23108 (measured 0.2312, error 0.04%)

label: IX.5
statement: Portal coupling: kappa = (Q/rho)^2 = 0.84919

label: IX.6
statement: Bistability: kappa^2/(4*lam3*lam4) = 4.0672 > 1

label: IX.7
statement: Gravity tree floor: 15^2 - 15 - 1 = 209

label: IX.8
statement: Gravity corrected floor: 15^2 - 1 = 224 = 14 x 16

label: IX.10
statement: Screening: lam4^2 = 0.03269858 = (2Q-1)/Q^2 (exact algebraic identity)

label: IX.11
statement: Barbero-Immirzi: gamma = lam4*rho = 0.239545 (LQG 0.2375, error 0.86%)

label: IX.12
statement: Ashtekar: rho <-> self-dual (N=+1), Q <-> anti-self-dual (N=-1)

label: IX.13
statement: Error hierarchy: rho*Q formulas most precise, pure-Q least precise

label: IX.14
statement: G = 6.6741e-11 from floor 224 + screening correction (error 0.003%)

label: X.1
statement: x^2=x+1 (n=2) -> phi = 1.61803 (Fibonacci/golden ratio, AutoMath seed)

label: X.2
statement: x^3=x+1 (n=3) -> rho = 1.32472 (Padovan, PDT classical sector)

label: X.3
statement: x^4=x+1 (n=4) -> Q = 1.22074 (quartic, PDT quantum sector)

label: X.4
statement: rho*Q = 1.61714 ~ phi = 1.61803: gap 0.000892 encodes Newton's constant

label: X.5
statement: N(phi)=-1 in Q(sqrt(5)) and N(rho*Q)=-1 in Q(rho,Q): same norm structure

label: X.6
statement: phi^2=phi+1 (exact); (rho*Q)^2 != rho*Q+1 (gap encodes non-Pisot cost)

label: X.7
statement: AutoMath (Omega Institute) derived ~2350 theorems from x^2=x+1 in Lean 4

label: X.8
statement: PDT uses n=3 AND n=4: the two members straddling the unique Pisot boundary

label: X.9
statement: AutoMath's seed (x^2=x+1) is the n=2 special case of the PDT family

label: X.10
statement: Next: port PDT branches to Lean 4 using Omega Institute's infrastructure
